Patch Set 1:

This was previously reverted because of the mdtest performance drop reported (by DDN) in LU-5197. So I'm curious, do you have mdtest number before and after applying this test to master? Or to put it differently, once this change is landed, will DDN ask for it to be reverted later?

Patch Set 1:

> This was previously reverted because of the mdtest performance drop
 > reported (by DDN) in LU-5197. So I'm curious, do you have mdtest
 > number before and after applying this test to master? Or to put it
 > differently, once this change is landed, will DDN ask for it to be
 > reverted later?

Hi John, yeah, previous one was reverted because of performance regression, this one is updated as Jinshan suggested, to avoid to introduce regression. Even my local testing looks fine, but still, I have pinged guys in community to test it more before moving forward.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

Beside LU-8047, you could possibly find our versions of LU-4398 useful: https://pastebin.com/GHj0rqxT

I'm also removing my +1 since this commit seems to have removed a few bits from the original patch which seems essential.

Patch Set 1:

Andrew, yes, the test no longer reproduces the issue after the open lock changes in llite from https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/20354/.

Gu Zheng, could you explain the situation where this change will be useful? Does it involve NFS export?

Patch Set 2:

John, you are right. A small correction, however: your original reproducer stopped working after LU-4367, not after LU-8019. Before LU-4367, the first execution of echo would cache the lookup lock and the second execution would use a cached dentry, go through ll_file_open() and request MDS_OPEN_LOCK. However, LU-4367 modified ll_revalidate_dentry() in such a way that revalidation fails for open requests, so the client would request open from the common (non-NFS) lookup path without MDS_OPEN_LOCK, not from ll_file_open().

Please note that I pasted a link to pastebin with the improved version of LU-4398 and updated test which we're still using for 2.5.

However, due to our migration to a later Lustre version we are also interested in LU-4398 being landed upstream. We encountered this bug only with NFS configurations in a scenario such as uploaded to pastebin.

Thank you

Patch Set 2:

This patch should be able to fix the problem, but writing a test case for NFS is awkward. I would suggest to delete the test case and submit the patch again.

Patch Set 3:

Perhaps Andrew has a different philosophy, but I think it's good to keep the test even though it passes *before* and after the code change. The test is correct and would guard against a future regression in this area.

Patch Set 3:

John, I have no objection to this test case alone. However, landing it together with the fix for NFS (it's what's broken now and requires fixing) makes the wrong impression that the non-NFS test is somehow related to the fix, e.g. it can make the wrong impression that the fix was formally verifed when landed.

Patch Set 3:

In fact, in the context of this review request it's not even clear whether the author ran any tests before uploading the latest patch set at all.

Patch Set 3:

Hi Gu Zhang,

Would it be possible to define a fail_loc to simulate the NFS test case?

commit.subject: LU-4398 mdt: acquire an open lock for write or execute
commit.message:
LU-4398 mdt: acquire an open lock for write or execute

In mdt_object_open_lock() opens for write or execute will always
acquire an open lock of the appropriate mode so that any conflicting
cached open locks on other clients will be canceled. Add a regression
test to sanityn.sh.

Change-Id: I88ad77d8b04da5017544082b8780b3f11f959494
Suggested-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@uber.com>
Signed-off-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Gu Zheng <gzheng@ddn.com>
